data.title: Addex Therapeutics Earnings Call: Strategic Gains Amid Challenges
data.description:
The recent earnings call for Addex Therapeutics Ltd (ADR) presented a balanced sentiment, highlighting both significant achievements and notable challenges. The company showcased its progress in strategic partnerships and financial stability, while also addressing decreased income and increased net loss from associated ventures. The call also pointed out the limited cash available for unpartnered programs, painting a comprehensive picture of the company’s current standing.

Successful Completion of IND-Enabling Studies

Addex Therapeutics marked a significant milestone with the successful completion of IND-enabling studies for a GABAB PAM drug candidate aimed at substance use disorders. This achievement is a key development in their partnership with Indivior, underscoring the progress in their collaborative efforts.

Regained Rights to mGlu2 Program

In a strategic move, Addex regained the rights to the mGlu2 positive allosteric modulator program from Johnson & Johnson. This includes the Phase II asset ADX71149, positioning Addex to explore new therapeutic indications and potentially expand their market reach.

Investment in Stalicla

Addex has invested in Stalicla, a company focused on neurodevelopmental disorders. Stalicla’s precision medicine platform for patient stratification aligns with Addex’s strategic interests, potentially enhancing their capabilities in personalized medicine.

Financial Stability

The company reported a cash reserve of CHF 2.3 million, providing financial stability and a runway through mid-2026. This financial position is crucial for sustaining operations and supporting ongoing projects.

Reduced Cash Burn

Following the Neurosterix spin-out transaction, Addex significantly reduced its cash burn. This strategic move is expected to enhance financial efficiency and allocate resources more effectively.

Decrease in Income

Addex experienced a decrease in income by CHF 0.1 million in Q2 2025 compared to the previous year. This decline is primarily attributed to the completion of the funded research phase with Indivior, highlighting the need for new revenue streams.

Increased Net Loss from Neurosterix

The company’s share of net loss from its 20% participation in Neurosterix Group increased by CHF 0.7 million, totaling CHF 1.2 million for Q2 2025. This increase reflects the financial challenges associated with their investment in Neurosterix.

Limited Cash for Unpartnered Programs

Addex faces a financial constraint as the current cash reserves do not support the advancement of unpartnered programs into the clinic. This limitation underscores the need for additional funding to progress these initiatives.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Looking ahead, Addex Therapeutics is optimistic about its GABAB PAM program, with the potential to receive up to USD 330 million in milestone payments from Indivior. The company is also advancing its independent GABAB PAM program for chronic cough, with promising preclinical results. However, they acknowledge the need for additional financing to initiate IND-enabling studies for this program. Financial projections indicate a runway through mid-2026, although this does not cover unpartnered program advancements.

In summary, the earnings call for Addex Therapeutics Ltd (ADR) reflected a balanced sentiment, highlighting both achievements and challenges. The company is making strides in strategic partnerships and financial management, yet faces hurdles in income generation and funding for unpartnered programs. As Addex navigates these challenges, its focus on strategic growth and financial stability remains clear.

data.title: YPF Approves AR$19 Billion Share Buyback Plan
data.description:
An announcement from YPF Sociedad Anonima ( (YPF) ) is now available.

On October 9, 2025, YPF Sociedad Anónima’s Board of Directors approved a plan to acquire up to AR$19,056,000,000 worth of its own shares. This move is aimed at implementing share compensation plans and is backed by a reserve fund of AR$34,205,000,000, ensuring the company’s liquidity and solvency remain intact. The acquisition will occur within 90 days, with a daily purchase limit set by market regulations, potentially impacting the company’s stock value and shareholder interests.

data.title: UTime Limited Reaffirms Leadership After Unauthorized Filings
data.description:
UTime ( (WTO) ) just unveiled an update.

UTime Limited recently faced unauthorized filings and press releases falsely indicating changes in its leadership, which were made by a former employee. On September 9, 2025, and September 11, 2025, these fraudulent actions were discovered, prompting UTime to reaffirm its current management and board composition, which remains unchanged. The company has taken steps to regain control of its Edgar filing codes and is working with the SEC to address the situation, ensuring transparency and protecting its stakeholders.

More about UTime

UTime Limited, trading under the NASDAQ ticker WTO, is a mobile device manufacturer and technology company engaged in the design, development, production, sales, and brand operation of mobile devices in China and globally. The company aims to provide cost-effective products to a broad customer base.

data.title: Cartica Acquisition Extends Business Combination Deadline
data.description:
An update from Cartica Acquisition ( (CRTAF) ) is now available.

Cartica Acquisition Corp has extended its Business Combination deadline from October 7, 2025, to February 7, 2026, by issuing a promissory note of $148,409.44 to its sponsor, Cartica Acquisition Partners, LLC. This extension allows the company more time to complete a merger or similar transaction, with monthly deposits into a trust account to support this process. During an extraordinary general meeting on October 3, 2025, shareholders approved the extension, ratified the appointment of CBIZ CPAs P.C. as the independent auditor for 2025, and agreed to adjourn the meeting if necessary. Following shareholder redemptions, approximately $5.2 million was withdrawn from the trust account, leaving 927,559 public shares outstanding. The company continues to manage its trust account funds in U.S. government securities until a business combination is consummated or the company is liquidated.

More about Cartica Acquisition

Average Trading Volume: 4,035

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $88.48M

For a thorough assessment of CRTAF stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:

On October 7, 2025, MannKind completed the acquisition of scPharma, making it a wholly-owned subsidiary. NexGen Energy ( (TSE:NXE) ) has issued an announcement.

On October 1, 2025, NexGen Energy Ltd. announced an equity financing initiative involving agreements with North American and Australian underwriters to raise approximately C$400 million and AUD$400 million, respectively, through the sale of common shares. The following day, the company announced an upsizing of the Australian offering to AUD$600 million. This strategic move aims to bolster NexGen’s financial position and support its ongoing projects, potentially enhancing its market standing and providing significant opportunities for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:NXE) stock is a Buy with a C$16.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NexGen Energy stock, see the TSE:NXE Stock Forecast page.

The valuation is constrained by a negative P/E ratio, reflecting the company’s current unprofitability. Recent corporate events provide positive long-term prospects, but are already considered in the earnings call.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:NXE stock, click here.

More about NexGen Energy

data.title: Carisma Therapeutics Faces Nasdaq Delisting and Trading Suspension
data.description:
Carisma Therapeutics ( (CARM) ) has provided an update.

On October 9, 2025, Carisma Therapeutics Inc. received a delisting determination letter from Nasdaq due to noncompliance with listing rules, resulting in the suspension of its common stock trading on Nasdaq effective October 13, 2025. The company plans to list its stock on the OTCID market tier but acknowledges uncertainties regarding continued trading and market-making. Carisma Therapeutics intends to sell or monetize its remaining assets and wind down operations, with little expectation of cash distribution to stockholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (CARM) stock is a Hold with a $0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Carisma Therapeutics stock, see the CARM Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Clover Health Announces 2026 Medicare Star Ratings
data.description:

On October 9, 2025, Clover Health Investments announced the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services’ 2026 Star Ratings for its Medicare Advantage plans, with its PPO plans receiving 3.5 Stars and its HMO plan receiving 4.0 Stars. Despite these ratings, Clover Health is confident in its ability to drive above-market membership growth and increasing profitability through its technology-centric care strategy and AI-driven improvements in its Clover Assistant platform. The company emphasizes that its business model can sustain growth and profitability independent of the Star ratings, as demonstrated by its strong revenue and membership growth in the first half of 2025. Clover Health also highlights its high clinical quality scores and ongoing engagement with CMS to ensure its ratings reflect its health outcomes.
